title: From The Whole To A Part
study: A Study In The Life Of John The
scriptureForWeek: VARIOUS PASSAGES IN THE GOSPELS Make sure you focus on the verses for each day, as these important events are taken from several readings of scripture across several chapters. These are chosen to highlight the ministry of John the Baptist and to frame up the gap between the Old and New Testament timelines.
weekTheme: Boys Whisper, Men Shout God will clarify your call. GOD HAS GOOD NEWS FOR THE WORLD. HE’S LOOKING FOR MEN WITH THE COURAGE TO SHARE IT. There are so many opinions in the world today. Between the news, celebrities, social media, our family, and friends, everyone wants their voice to be heard above all the noise. Everyone wants the people around them to think like them. But whose opinion is the right one? Everyone seems to have a different take on things, as little as which is the best sports team to as big as what men should do with their sexuality. There can be lots of confusion, lots of lies, and lots of distractions. Many of these opinions can be dangerous. They can be filled with lies and distortions that lead us away from God and into traps and temptations that fill us with shame and regret. God sends men into the world with his truth to break through the chaos and clamor of all the worldly opinions. He raises up men who will speak his truth, invite people to know God better, confront the lies, and call people to their destiny. This week, we will look at the message John the Baptist carried to his generation, and how God may want to use you to speak his truth to others. What God calls us to speak is important because it’s his opinion that really matters. God has something to say; get ready to raise your voice.

days.title: Raise Your Voice
days.scripture: MATTHEW 3:1-3 In those days John the Baptist came to the Judean wilderness and began preaching. His message was, “Repent of your sins and turn to God, for the Kingdom of Heaven is near.” The prophet Isaiah was speaking about John when he said, “He is a voice shouting in the wilderness, ‘Prepare the way for the LORD’s coming! Clear the road for him!’”
days.reflection: Have you ever been hanging out, living your normal life, when all of a sudden everyone’s cell phones start going off at the same time with a weird alarm? Those are the moments when the authorities break into our ordinary lives to warn us of something important that we need to pay attention to. Sometimes it’s a warning about a bad storm, sometimes it’s a warning about a missing person, or sometimes it’s a warning about a terrorist threat. The authorities of heaven break into our normal lives too. Instead of using cell phone warnings, God uses men. This is who John the Baptist was to his world. He was God's important announcement about an important event. After all the years of preparation in the wilderness, John heard God speak to him. God gave him a message to help get the people ready for a visit from heaven. John raised his voice and released a shout. Here is the core of his message. Repent and turn. To repent means to change the way you think about something and turn your life around. It's like seeing a sign when you are driving along that says “road closed ahead due to flooding, turn around and take a detour.” John warned people that following their own sinful ways would lead them to danger and disaster. He was giving them a loving warning to turn back to God to find forgiveness and hope. The kingdom is near. God’s main goal isn’t to get your soul to go to heaven when you die. It’s to get his kingdom to come to earth while you live. God’s kingdom is his power, authority, and will being done in the world. It’s like a new president coming into power and changing the broken and unjust laws to godly ones so that people can flourish. It isn’t just an airy-fairy vision but a real picture of peace and justice happening in the world through the people of God. John was getting everyone ready for Jesus to appear and set up God's kingdom on earth. Get ready for God to visit you. Have you ever had people over for dinner and been told to make sure your room was clean? Now imagine Jesus was coming over for dinner. How would you prepare for that? John was telling people that Jesus was coming and they needed to prepare for a heavenly guest. But instead of getting their homes ready, they were going to get their hearts ready. Bringing God's message into the world is a key part of what God wants you to do in your life. He has given you the truth of the gospel, put you in a world where people desperately need hope, and wants to use you to get others ready for God's kingdom. The good news is you don’t have to share your opinion. Just God's. You are sharing his word, not your own. That gives you confidence and humility to speak with passion and conviction, without being arrogant and dogmatic. We need messengers in the world like that. We need you to raise your voice.
questions: Who is one of your favorite Christian speakers or teachers you have ever listened to? What makes their speaking helpful for you?
If God could give a message to your generation, what do you think he might want to say?
Ask your Dad about one of the first times that God really got his attention. How did he hear God's voice, and what was God saying to him?

thoughts.verse: Live wisely among those who are not believers, and make the most of every opportunity. Let your conversation be gracious and attractive so that you will have the right response for everyone.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to give you a conviction about your faith. Ask him to give you a message of his love for your friends around you.

days.title: Let Your Life Speak
days.scripture: MATTHEW 3:4-6 John’s clothes were woven from coarse camel hair, and he wore a leather belt around his waist. For food he ate locusts and wild honey. People from Jerusalem and from all of Judea and all over the Jordan Valley went out to see and hear John. And when they confessed their sins, he baptized them in the Jordan River.
days.reflection: What we wear tells a lot about who we are. Some kids wear all black and dress like goths. What are they trying to communicate? Some girls wear skimpy clothes to get attention. What are they trying to communicate? Some guys are really picky with the brands they wear. What do those brands say? The way we dress can give a ton of clues as to who we are. Clothes can reveal if we are rich or poor, care about sports or video games, or even what part of the country we come from. Clothes can speak; they can be clues to a guy's values and calling. John the Baptist dressed in such a way as to speak to those around him. Today’s reading says, John’s clothes were woven from coarse camel hair, and he wore a leather belt around his waist. For food he ate locusts and wild honey. Clothes made from camel hair? That sounds kind of weird. Eating locusts? Yuck. A leather belt - not too bad. Honey, now that’s not too bad. John dressed like this to let people know he was a man on a mission with a message from God. And he was reminding them of another man of God in history who dressed this same way. Elijah. In 2 Kings 1:7-9 Elijah is described as a man who wore hairy clothes and had a leather belt around his waist. John wanted his lifestyle to be a reminder to his generation that God was on the move and they needed to prepare. He set himself apart in every area of his life so that he could play his part in the story of God. Most guys dress to fit in. John dressed to stand out. Most people live like everyone else so they will be accepted. John lived like men of God from old so he could stand out. The Bible tells us in tons of different ways to let our lives speak. The most important message you will ever give is how you live. It’s not just what you say, but how you live that stands out. You are called to be dressed differently than the world. You are called to dress in armor to fight. Ephesians 6:13 says: Therefore, put on every piece of God’s armor so you will be able to resist the enemy in the time of evil. Then after the battle you will still be standing firm. Stand your ground, putting on the belt of truth and the body armor of God’s righteousness. For shoes, put on the peace that comes from the Good News so that you will be fully prepared. In addition to all of these, hold up the shield of faith to stop the fiery arrows of the devil. Put on salvation as your helmet, and take the sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God. You are called to dress in love to care. Colossians 3:12-15 says this: Since God chose you to be the holy people he loves, you must clothe yourselves with tenderhearted mercy, kindness, humility, gentleness, and patience. Make allowance for each other’s faults, and forgive anyone who offends you. Remember, the Lord forgave you, so you must forgive others. Above all, clothe yourselves with love, which binds us all together in perfect harmony. God doesn’t want you to be a clone of the trends and habits of the world around you. He wants you to be different so you can make a difference. He wants to clothe you with love, armor, and power. Make sure you learn to dress properly.
questions: What is your favorite outfit? Why?
Can you think of a few ways that Christian guys are meant to be different in their lifestyle than those that are not believers?
What do you think happens when people who are Christians live no differently than the world? Have you ever seen this happen? What did it make you think?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share a defining moment when they knew God was asking them to live differently than the world around them. What was it, and what happened?

thoughts.verse: Dear friends, I warn you as “temporary residents and foreigners” to keep away from worldly desires that wage war against your very souls. Be careful to live properly among your unbelieving neighbors.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to help you live as an example to those around you. Ask him for his forgiveness and grace for any areas you have not been a faithful witness.

days.title: A Spiritual Showdown
days.scripture: MATTHEW 3:7-10 But when he saw many Pharisees and Sadducees coming to watch him baptize, he denounced them. “You brood of snakes!” he exclaimed. “Who warned you to flee the coming wrath? Prove by the way you live that you have repented of your sins and turned to God. Don’t just say to each other, ‘We’re safe, for we are descendants of Abraham.’ That means nothing, for I tell you, God can create children of Abraham from these very stones. Even now the ax of God’s judgment is poised, ready to sever the roots of the trees. Yes, every tree that does not produce good fruit will be chopped down and thrown into the fire.
days.reflection: Do you like conflict? Are you happy to call people out when they are doing the wrong thing? When you are around your friends and they want to be disobedient, do you stand up and challenge them, or shrink back in silence out of fear of rejection? Confronting other people is hard, really hard. Particularly for guys your age. But men have to learn to confront what is wrong. If men don’t stand up against hypocrites and hurtful people, the world around them can be filled with fear and pain. God needs men to confront what’s wrong in the world. To take your place in the story of God, you are going to have to ask God for courage to confront. In John the Baptist's day, the Pharisees and Sadducees were the most popular and powerful rulers amongst the Jewish people. Yet they had grown corrupt. They used their power for themselves and were making up their own religious rules that God didn’t care about. This was beating the people down and causing them deep discouragement. When John shows up, he goes straight in for a fight. Look at how intense his language was. Could you ever imagine saying this to religious rulers? John calls them snakes. He says they are not godly leaders but a brood of vipers. He warns them that the axe is at the root of the tree, ready to be chopped down. Can you imagine someone standing up in church and saying this to a local pastor? People would freak out. It takes courage to confront what is wrong in the world. For guys your age, it may be one of the hardest things of all. But we need men willing to do this. Believe it or not, God is shaping you to be a man with the courage to confront what’s wrong and call for what’s right. It may start small, like sticking up for a kid being bullied, and grow as you get older, like taking on systems, structures, and policies that are unjust, but you will need courage like John the Baptist. You are in a story about God making the world right. You are in a story where Jesus will come again to make all things new. But for now, he needs men to help get things ready. That’s why you are here. Pray for courage. Confrontation is coming.
questions: On a scale of 1-10, (one being the lowest) how well do you tend to handle confronting people? How do you feel when people confront you?
How do you think people your age tend to handle being confronted when they are wrong? Why do you think they act this way?
Ask the person you are doing this study with about a time someone confronted them and it went well, and a time someone confronted them and it didn’t.

thoughts.verse: Speak up for those who cannot speak for themselves; ensure justice for those being crushed. Yes, speak up for the poor and helpless, and see that they get justice.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to fill you with courage to stand up against those with power who are using it in the wrong way. Ask him for a chance to do this today.

days.title: Getting Ready
days.scripture: MATTHEW 3:11-12 “I baptize with water those who repent of their sins and turn to God. But someone is coming soon who is greater than I am—so much greater that I’m not worthy even to be his slave and carry his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire. He is ready to separate the chaff from the wheat with his winnowing fork. Then he will clean up the threshing area, gathering the wheat into his barn but burning the chaff with never-ending fire.”
days.reflection: Do you know what a narcissist is? You have probably heard that word thrown around a bit. It’s a person who is obsessed with themselves. They only think of what they need, how they look, and how they feel. They manipulate people and situations to focus on themselves. We live in a world that’s pretty narcissistic. Everyone wants to be the center of attention. They get obsessed with how many likes they get on social media or how popular they are at school. One of the greatest tensions you will ever face as a man is the tension between your own importance and the importance of Jesus. We can tend to see God as a character in our story, where we are the center. But this is wrong and prideful. The truth is, we are characters in God's story. He is the center. It's hard for young guys to believe this. It’s so hard to know you matter, but that your greatness is found in pointing to another: Jesus. John managed this tension really well. He was strong, courageous, and confident, and he didn’t care what people thought of him. He was rooted and secure. (Can you imagine being like that? :)) Yet, he knew his role in the story. He was a part of something really cool, but not the center. So, when the main character arrived, he was happy to give him the attention and point others to Jesus. The reason John believed that Jesus was greater was clear. John was just a person, even though he came from a wild background. But Jesus was the eternal son of God who took on flesh to save us from our sins. John was God's man, but Jesus was the God man. John baptized with water, which was symbolic and temporary. Jesus baptized in the Holy Spirit and fire, which is transforming and eternal. John came to prepare for God to move; Jesus was God moving amongst the people. John came to get things ready; Jesus came to get things done. There is a wonderful joy in knowing you have a part to play in the story of God, but that the story doesn’t have to depend on you. You can be secure in who God has made you and point others to Jesus without feeling left out. Confidence and humility are a winning combination. We see this in John, and God wants to work this in your life. You have a role, but Jesus is the center. Remember that.
questions: Have you ever been in a room with someone more important than you? How did it make you feel?

Take a moment to look up the dictionary definition of narcissism. In what ways can you see this in your generation? What kinds of things do you think you can do to guard against this creeping into your heart?
Ask your dad who the most important person is that he has ever met. Who was it, and how did it make him feel in his presence?

thoughts.verse: Because of the privilege and authority God has given me, I give each of you this warning: Don’t think you are better than you really are. Be honest in your evaluation of yourselves, measuring yourselves by the faith God has given us.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you from it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to make you both humble and confident. Confess any areas you feel insecure about to him, and ask him to strengthen his love in your heart.

days.title: Serious Separation
days.scripture: MATTHEW 3:11-12 “I baptize with water those who repent of their sins and turn to God. But someone is coming soon who is greater than I am—so much greater that I’m not worthy even to be his slave and carry his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire. He is ready to separate the chaff from the wheat with his winnowing fork. Then he will clean up the threshing area, gathering the wheat into his barn but burning the chaff with never-ending fire.”
days.reflection: One of the greatest lies Satan ever told the world is that God will never judge anyone. People are free to do what they want, without consequence. God is so nice and kind that in the end he will just let everyone off for what they have done wrong and all will be well. But happily ever after is for fairy tales, not for the kingdom of God. In The Screwtape Letters (a book I hope you will read when you are a bit older), C.S. Lewis pretends to be a demon writing to a younger demon. In it, the younger demon wants the Christian he is trying to tempt to commit a massive, terrible sin. But the older demon knows better. You don’t need big sins if small things will do. He writes. “It does not matter how small the sins are provided that their cumulative effect is to edge the man away from the Light and out into the Nothing. Murder is no better than cards if cards can do the trick. Indeed the safest road to Hell is the gradual one-the gentle slope, soft underfoot, without sudden turnings, without milestones, without signposts.” John came to warn people that Jesus was going to bring separation. Jesus was going to separate the proud from the humble, the self-righteous from the contrite, and the hungry from the hypocrite. He was not going to do this out of rage but out of love. John knew that Jesus was radically opposed to anything that oppressed, hurt, and dishonored God's people. He was the good and kind shepherd coming to chase off the wolves who were tearing God's sheep apart. People in our world need to know that God is going to take action to confront what is wrong. When Jesus came, he overthrew the tables in the temple out of anger for what they had done to his Father's house. He rebuked the Pharisees and called them sons of hell because they had created rules that he never intended that crushed God's people. Yet he did this with love. He wept over Jerusalem and died to give his life for others. If you are going to take your place in the story of God, you are going to have to learn to warn people about the consequences of their actions. Not like a goody- two-shoes who is self-righteous and thinks they are better than anyone else, but out of love and care for them to bring them back to the way of Jesus. This week, you have seen that God raised up John the Baptist with a call to prepare people’s hearts to meet Jesus. He had a message, a lifestyle, humility, and courage that caused people to pay attention. But when he got the attention, he gave it over to Jesus. Young man, believe it or not, you have a calling like John the Baptist. God wants to use you as his messenger to bring good news to the people around you. He wants to give you the truth of his word, love for others, courage to tell the truth, and a deep love for Jesus so that you live to honor him. Most guys your age have never really given this much thought, but God has chosen you to be a part of what he is doing in this generation. You don’t have to have it all figured out now, but you are right on track. Begin to open your eyes. Begin to see why your Dad is taking the time to invest and train you through the Primal Path. The world is waiting for good news, and part of that message will come through you.
questions: Why do you think Jesus gets so angry with the religious people in the bible, but is so kind to sinners? How is this different from how most people view God?
Being honest, how does it make you feel to think that God may want to use you as one of his messengers?
Ask your Dad if he has ever had the chance to share the good news of Jesus and God's kingdom with someone. Who was it, and how did it go? How did he feel when speaking to the person?

thoughts.verse: And all of this is a gift from God, who brought us back to himself through Christ. And God has given us this task of reconciling people to him. For God was in Christ, reconciling the world to himself, no longer counting people’s sins against them. And he gave us this wonderful message of reconciliation. So we are Christ’s ambassadors; God is making his appeal through us. We speak for Christ when we plead, “Come back to God!”
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to give you a vision of being one of his messengers. Ask him to take away any fear or doubt and to fill you with confidence and trust.

extras: WEEK 4 MANSCHOOL SOMETHING TO SAY MANSCHOOL REMINDER The recipe for fun is pretty simple when you’re raising boys: add to any activity an element of danger, stir in a little exploration, add a dash of destruction, and you’ve got yourself a winner. - John Eldredge OPTION 1. ACTIVITY: SOMETHING TO SAY Learning to speak in public is one of the greatest fears men have. But learning to articulate well and persuade others is massively important. This week, your son is going to prepare a short speech recapping what he has learned on the Primal Path so far, and then he will present it to your family, Work with him to compose an outline. (Here is a classic.) Introduction. What I am going to say. Why it matters. Lesson 1. Point. Bible Verse. Story. Lesson 2. Point. Bible Verse. Story. Lesson 3. Point. Bible Verse. Story.
FIELD NOTES PRAYER FOR MY SON
